failure_modes.text: System 1 can quietly take over and build a coherent story from limited evidence, leading the team to mistake fluency for truth.
failure_modes.source_quote: System 1 is generally useful and appropriate for navigating daily life most of the time, allowing us to be guided by impressions and feelings.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the evidence behind any conclusion that feels fluent and obvious; System 1 confidence tracks story quality, not evidence completeness.

failure_modes.text: Teams can treat familiar frameworks as if they are always the right tool, substituting a handy model for the real structure of the problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: if the only tool you have is a hammer, to treat everything as if it were a nail
failure_modes.mitigation: Test multiple frameworks against the same problem; if only one was considered, substitution bias may have smuggled in the wrong model.

failure_modes.text: Smart people can become better at narrative defense than at truth-seeking, creating an intelligence trap instead of protection from bias.
failure_modes.source_quote: high cognitive ability does not attenuate the bias blind spot
failure_modes.mitigation: Require explicit reasoning chains from smart contributors; intelligence amplifies rationalization as much as it amplifies truth-seeking.

premortem_questions.text: What sources of information have we missed or minimized because they do not fit the story we already prefer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What sources of information could I have missed or minimized on the way to reaching my belief?

premortem_questions.text: If an outside view replaced our inside view here, what base rates or denominator checks would it force us to confront?
premortem_questions.source_quote: To fight biases rooted in the inside view decisions must be anchored to the outside view

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe for the opposite thesis to be true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Force an outside-view check before committing to a plan that feels intuitively compelling.
heuristics.source_quote: decisions must be anchored to the outside view

heuristics.text: Use thesis, antithesis, and synthesis to make disconfirming evidence part of the process rather than a late objection.
heuristics.source_quote: use team norms like the Dialectic Standard (thesis, antithesis, synthesis)

heuristics.text: Document what was known at decision time so hindsight does not rewrite the learning afterward.
heuristics.source_quote: Use the Knowledge Tracker to separate what was known at the time of the decision from what was subsequently learned.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on diagnosing distorted judgment and forcing explicit debiasing moves rather than listing every named bias.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
